rag_rules: Assume statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Validator diagnostics pointing to implementation-level references in type annexes require moving valid bindings to implementation annexes or deleting invalid clauses
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must respect AADL scoping rules: type-level assumptions/guarantees reference type-level features only
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only visible identifiers from the model
Implementation-level bindings and assignments belong in component implementation annexes, not type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments
anti_patterns: Placing assume statements in component implementation scopes instead of component type scopes
Referencing implementation-level subcomponents or ports in component type contracts
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics for model fusion or requirement traceability
AGREE syntax for component type structure extraction
AGREE contract generation for requirement analysis
